task_completion.description: The Transforms module is currently open and visible in the module panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module panel title displays 'Transforms' or the Transforms module icon is highlighted in the toolbar/menu.

task_completion.description: A new LinearTransform node has been created and is selected as the Active Transform.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Active Transform' dropdown menu, a node is selected that is not 'None' or 'Scene' (e.g., named 'LinearTransform', 'HeadTiltCorrection', or similar).

task_completion.description: The rotation parameters are set to approximately 15 degrees around the IS (Inferior-Superior) axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Rotation' section of the Transforms module, the slider labeled 'IS' (or the third slider corresponding to the Z-axis) shows a value near 15.0 or -15.0. Alternatively, the transformation matrix shows changes consistent with Z-axis rotation.

task_completion.description: The MRHead volume is associated with the transform (moved to the Transformed list).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Apply transform' section at the bottom of the module, the 'Transformed' list box (usually on the right) contains 'MRHead'. The 'Transformable' list (left) does not contain it.

task_completion.description: The slice views visually confirm the brain volume is rotated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The brain anatomy in the slice views (Red/Yellow/Green windows) appears tilted or rotated relative to the image frame, consistent with the applied 15-degree rotation.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor, highlighted buttons, or open menus within the Slicer GUI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use the Python console to script the solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console is not the primary focus, and there is no evidence of setting matrix values via code commands.

integrity.description: The screenshots depict the actual 3D Slicer application environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Slicer layout (Module panel, 3D view, Slice views) is clearly visible and authentic.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the 'MRHead' volume. The target transform is a rotation of ~15 degrees around the IS (Inferior-Superior) axis. In 3D Slicer's Transforms module, this is typically controlled by the third rotation slider (labeled IS). The 'Apply transform' section must show 'MRHead' in the 'Transformed' column (right side) to be valid. The transform should not be hardened (applied permanently), so the association must remain visible in the GUI.
